metadata.title: SYNGO_BP_1845
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Fig.4: TrkC and PTPσ Ectodomains Induce Excitatory Presynaptic and Postsynaptic Differentiation, Respectively. Experimental description: 7/11/2017 Pim
- Species of cultured neurons is not explicitly stated in the paper.
- Species of TrkC-Fc is rat species: "The cDNAs for full-length rat TrkCTK- ... were cloned by RT-PCR from a P11 rat brain cDNA library"

Await species information (amcraig@interchange.ubc.ca , dd 7/11/2017)

8/11/2017 Pim
- Authors responded:
TrkC-Fc protein were purchased from R&D systems ( https://www.rndsystems.com/products/recombinant-human-trkc-fc-chimera-protein-cf_373-tc-cf ). Thus, it's a human TrkC.
(We also used recombinant proteins of TrkCN366AT369A-Fc in Supplemental Figure S2. This mutated TrkC-Fc is based on rat TrkC (BC078844).)
neuronal cultures, we used rat primary hippocampal neuron cultures.

- The authors use a combination of rat cells, and human and rat TrkC. I annotate against the rat protein and cells.

checks.error_message: No inferences attributed to this model graph in the materializer output. Likely a parse failure or other materializer skip — re-run on this model alone to investigate.
